source.description: A reviewed six-panel marker process contact sheet.
source.notes: One face-free upright rocket on textured drawing paper.

process_strategy: The contact sheet keeps one upright rocket while building the body guide, nose and fins, window, flame, black outline, marker fills, and final clarity pass.

final_elements.name: upright rocket body guide
final_elements.description: A tall rounded body with a center axis.

final_elements.name: nose cone and side fins
final_elements.description: A pointed nose and two matching triangular fins.

final_elements.name: round window and lower band
final_elements.description: A porthole and simple horizontal body band.

final_elements.name: flame plume and small stars
final_elements.description: A layered flame below the rocket with two small orbiting star marks.

final_elements.name: red, cyan, yellow, and purple marker color
final_elements.description: Saturated fills on existing body parts and simple shadow.

frames.visible_job: Places the tall rounded rocket guide and center axis.
Adds the nose cone and matching side fins to the guide.
Adds the porthole and lower band on the established body.
Adds a layered flame and small star marks around the existing rocket.
Thickens existing outlines and fills the rocket and flame with marker color.
introduces: red, cyan, yellow, and purple marker color

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens established contours, fills, stars, and flame.

rejection_checks: No fin, window, band, flame, star, color, or highlight may first appear in the final image.
The rocket must retain one upright pose, scale, and placement in every frame.
No late panel may turn the marker drawing into a glossy vector icon or add a face, logo, or extra prop.
